This represents an ~11x increase from baseline. Such a massive spike would require an extraordinary, unprecedented crisis in Honduras. While upcoming elections (Nov 30, 2025) create some heightened tension and political activity, they are extremely unlikely to cause a 10x increase in strategic developments. The probability is very low across all three dates, with slight increases for later dates reflecting greater uncertainty about future crisis scenarios.

The reference value of 1.000 means the 30-day average of strategic developments in Lesotho over the past year is approximately 0. For YES resolution, there must be MORE than 10 strategic developments in the 30-day window before each resolution date. This requires an extraordinary spike from near-zero to 11+ events in a single month. Lesotho shows no signs of major political crisis in late 2025, and the country's current trajectory appears relatively stable (no coup, no elections). Going from essentially 0 events/month to 11+ would require an unprecedented escalation. Probabilities are set very low (3%, 4%, 5%) with slight increases for longer forecast horizons reflecting greater uncertainty further out.

The baseline of 128.25 protests/month is the recent average. Key factors: (1) Protests have been trending upward (from ~113/month avg through Aug 2024 to 128.25/month avg through Oct 2025), suggesting current levels are near or above the baseline. (2) Weekly demonstrations have been consistently maintained throughout 2024-2025 driven by the hostage crisis, Gaza war, and anti-Netanyahu sentiment. (3) August 2025 saw some of the largest protests of the war period. (4) No resolution to the underlying drivers is in sight. For Nov 2 (7 days ahead): ~63% - recent high protest activity is likely to continue in the 30-day window just ending. For Nov 25 (~30 days ahead): ~58% - slightly more uncertainty but structural conditions favor continued elevated protests. For Jan 24, 2026 (~3 months ahead): ~52% - holiday season (late Dec-Jan) may dampen protests somewhat, increased baseline uncertainty over longer horizon.

The DRC conflict is highly active as of the knowledge cutoff (Oct 26, 2025), with the UN envoy confirming the ceasefire is not being respected, M23 killing 39 civilians in one week (Oct 6-12), and ongoing operations by multiple armed groups. The reference average of 11.25 events/30 days already reflects the elevated conflict period in 2025 (including the highly intense early 2025 period). Current violence levels appear near or above the average baseline, giving a moderately elevated probability of exceeding it. The October 14 Doha monitoring mechanism is a positive diplomatic step but doesn't constitute an actual ceasefire, and historically DRC peace deals have repeatedly failed to hold. Probability decreases with horizon as diplomatic progress is possible over longer timeframes, but remains above 50% across all dates given structural conflict dynamics.

The baseline of 3.167 protests/30 days was elevated by the intense Sept-Oct 2024 unrest. By late 2025, the RPPRAC movement has transitioned toward political party building (party founded Oct 2025), reducing street protest frequency. Activity in 2025 includes a Paris march (July), Matinik Tour (August), and site tensions (September), but intensity appears lower than the 2024 surge. For Nov 2, 2025 (closest date), I estimate ~38% probability of exceeding the elevated baseline. For Nov 25 and Jan 24, uncertainty increases toward 0.5, reflecting longer forecast horizons and potential for either escalation or continued decline in protest activity.

The threshold requires ≥32 'Strategic developments' in Hungary in a 30-day period (10 × 3.167 = 31.67), which is approximately 15x the normal monthly rate of ~2.17. Hungary is a stable EU democracy with no armed conflict. While politically active (Orbán vs. Magyar/TISZA rivalry, 2026 elections), political protests are classified under 'Protests' category in ACLED, not 'Strategic developments'. Reaching 32 strategic developments in a month would require catastrophic destabilization (coup, constitutional crisis, near-conflict) with no current signs. Probability is very low across all resolution dates, with slight increase for the most distant date due to greater uncertainty.

Probabilities increase modestly with time horizon per forecasting conventions.

The reference value is 20.167 strategic developments per 30-day period. This active conflict environment generates many strategic developments (military deployments, administrative changes, humanitarian coordination challenges). Near-term windows (Nov 2, Nov 25) are very likely to exceed the average given ongoing escalation. The Jan 2026 window has higher uncertainty due to longer forecast horizon - the conflict may continue at elevated levels but natural regression to mean or potential stabilization is more possible. Probabilities decrease with forecast horizon as instructed.

Venezuela has chronic internal violence from colectivos and armed groups, but no evidence suggests a 10x+ spike is imminent. Historical ACLED data for Venezuela does not show events near this threshold even during peak repression periods. Probabilities are low across all horizons, with slight increase for longer horizons due to greater uncertainty about potential escalation scenarios.

The baseline of 31.167 ACLED fatalities per 30-day period already reflects the elevated violence of the past year in Bangladesh (post-August 2024 political transition). Key factors: (1) Political violence at a 4-year high with ongoing intra-party clashes, (2) Rohingya camp violence (ARSA activity) contributes regularly, (3) General homicide rate up 25.9% in H1 2025, (4) Political uncertainty under interim government persists. However, since the baseline incorporates the current elevated period, the probability of any given 30-day window exceeding the average is approximately 50%. The slight upward lean (55%) for near-term dates reflects the continuing upward trend in violence during 2025. The Jan 2026 date is pulled closer to 50% due to greater uncertainty over the longer horizon.

Mongolia's ACLED reference value is 1.000 (essentially zero fatalities per 30-day period over the past year), requiring 11+ deaths in 30 days for YES resolution. Mongolia is historically very peaceful with near-zero ACLED-recorded fatalities. Current political turmoil (PM ousted, constitutional crisis) involves entirely non-violent political/parliamentary processes with no reported fatalities. Even during Mongolia's worst historical incidents (2008 riots), only 5 deaths occurred. The probability of 11+ ACLED-recorded fatalities in any 30-day window is extremely low (~3-5%), increasing slightly with longer forecast horizons per the uncertainty principle.

The reference 30-day average of 435.417 protests was calculated over 360 days that included Turkey's largest protest wave in a decade (March 2025 Imamoğlu arrest) and September 2025 CHP crackdown protests, making it an elevated baseline. Current protest activity (October 2025) is ongoing but at lower intensity than the March peak - government has intensified restrictions, seized opposition media, and protests appear smaller (courthouse protest ~1,000 people on Oct 26 vs. hundreds of thousands in March). The political tensions (Imamoğlu espionage charges, CHP crackdown) keep protest activity alive but government suppression limits scale. For the near-term dates (Nov 2, Nov 25), slightly below 50% probability due to the elevated baseline. For the distant date (Jan 24, 2026), uncertainty increases, moves toward 50%.

The baseline 30-day average of 'Explosions/Remote violence' in Chile is 0.083, representing approximately 1 event per year over the preceding 360 days. For resolution to be YES, at least 1 event must occur in any 30-day window (since any integer ≥ 1 exceeds 0.083). Using a Poisson model: P(X≥1) = 1 - e^(-0.083) ≈ 8%. Chile has ongoing low-intensity conflict in Araucania and occasional anarchist activity, but ACLED strictly categorizes explosions/remote violence (requiring at-range weapons), filtering out many incidents. No specific high-risk anniversary dates fall in the relevant windows. I slightly adjust upward from the pure Poisson estimate to account for ongoing conflict and uncertainty, and push increasingly toward 0.50 for longer forecast horizons.

The reference value of 1.083 implies ~1 strategic development per year in New Caledonia (30-day average = 0.083). The 6-month Treasury yield has been declining sharply (from 4.46% in Oct 2024 to ~3.76% by Oct 24, 2025) driven by Fed rate cut expectations. The FRED model gives P(exceed)=0.68 for Nov 2, but this uses a longer trend that predicts mean reversion to 3.78. However, (1) recent momentum is clearly downward, (2) Reuters (Oct 15) explicitly states "short-dated US Treasury yields will edge lower on Fed rate cut expectations," and (3) the Fed is expected to continue cutting through Q1 2026, which directly reduces 6-month yields. I adjust the FRED model's estimate downward to account for these factors. For the near term (Nov 2), noise and randomness keep the probability at ~0.42; for longer horizons, the structural downward trend and continued easing cycle push the probability increasingly below 0.50. Uncertainty increases with forecast horizon as specified.

The resolution criteria requires new/expanded export controls (not China-specific) with an effective date between May 1 and December 31, 2025, verifiable through Federal Register, EAR updates, or official BIS/Commerce announcements.

Key evidence: 
1) The Trump administration rescinded the Biden-era AI Diffusion Rule in May 2025 (removing rather than expanding controls)
2) BIS issued guidance documents (on Huawei chips, knowledge requirements) in May 2025, but these aren't formal regulations
3) The replacement framework rule was only in proposed rule stage as of September 2025, with its August 2025 NPRM target already missed
4) China-specific chip restrictions (e.g., H20) don't qualify per resolution criteria
5) BIS has signaled more rules are coming and has historically used fast-tracked IFRs

The probability balances: the clear intent to implement new rules and BIS's ability to use IFRs (which skip the normal NPRM process) against the reality that as of late September 2025 (~3 months before deadline), no qualifying rule had been published and the replacement was still in proposal stage.

As of October 2025, no US federal legislation has been passed and signed into law that substantively regulates AI systems or their development under the resolution criteria. The TAKE IT DOWN Act (signed May 19, 2025) addresses AI-generated content publication but does not regulate AI systems or their development (as explicitly noted in the question background). The One Big Beautiful Bill was signed into law but without the AI moratorium provision. No comprehensive federal AI regulatory framework is near passage, the Trump administration is focused on deregulation, and there are only ~2 months remaining before the deadline. The probability of qualifying legislation passing and being signed into law before January 1, 2026 is very low (~5%).

As of October 24, 2025 (2 days before the knowledge cutoff), the Coalition of the Willing explicitly stated that deployment plans are contingent on hostilities ceasing. No ceasefire or peace deal exists, Russia-Ukraine fighting continues, and internal coalition disagreements remain. Even with a sudden ceasefire, deploying 15,000-50,000 troops takes months. With only ~2 months before the December 31, 2025 resolution date, actual deployment under the Coalition of the Willing framework in 2025 is extremely unlikely.

Multiple European countries (UK, Germany, France, Italy, Norway) have already formally committed to providing M270 MLRS systems (explicitly listed as HIMARS-compatible in the resolution criteria) to Ukraine since 2022, with deliveries ongoing as of 2025. As of March 2025, approximately 25-34 M270s had been provided by these European nations. In April 2025, allies pledged a record €21 billion in new military aid at the Ramstein summit, with the UK committing £4.5 billion for 2025 specifically. A September 2025 article confirms Europe's continued "dash to procure rocket artillery." Given that the resolution criteria requires only ONE formal announcement from any European nation or the EU, and multiple countries have already made such commitments (which remain active through 2025), this question resolves YES with near-certainty. The maximum probability is capped at 0.98 to account for the theoretical possibility of definitional disputes about whether existing commitments need to be re-announced.

Based on Bruegel data and reporting: Q1 2025 EU imports from Russia were ~10.5 bcm (the highest quarter), Q2 was ~8.8 bcm, and Q3 appears to be ~7-8 bcm. The 15 bcm threshold is approximately 50% above the highest 2025 quarterly figure. Infrastructure limitations (TurkStream at capacity, Ukraine pipeline closed), policy pressure, and declining trend make it virtually impossible for any 2025 quarter to reach 15 bcm. Even Q4 2025 with seasonal winter demand increase would at most approach 11-12 bcm, far below the threshold.

As of late October 2025, Elon Musk is firmly #1 on the Bloomberg Billionaires Index with a net worth of ~$453-645 billion (various estimates), far ahead of #2 Larry Ellison at ~$367 billion. Musk became the world's first person to surpass $500 billion in early October 2025. The only interruption to his #1 status was a brief ~1-day period in September 2025 when Ellison momentarily surpassed him. With an enormous lead (100+ billion dollars), it would take extraordinary market events to dislodge him by December 31, 2025. The main risks are a major Tesla stock crash or dramatic Oracle/competitor surge, but given the substantial gap, the probability of Musk remaining #1 is very high.

Iran has never closed or severely restricted the Strait of Hormuz despite decades of threats. In June 2025, the US bombed Iranian nuclear facilities, triggering Iran's parliament to approve a Strait closure measure, and Iran made mining preparations - but a ceasefire was reached by June 25. Post-ceasefire, shipping traffic recovered and no significant disruptions occurred. As of October 2025, the Strait remains open. Fresh October threats are conditional/leverage-based rather than imminent action. Strong US military deterrence, economic self-harm (Iran's own oil exports transit the Strait), opposition from China/Russia, and the historical base rate of zero actual closures all argue strongly against resolution as YES. The remaining ~2 months of the window at a fragile period adds some upside risk, but probability remains low at approximately 8%.

The major India-Pakistan military conflict of 2025 (Operation Sindoor, May 7-10) already occurred and ended with a ceasefire without any formal declaration of war. India historically has never issued a formal declaration of war, even during its major conflicts in 1962, 1965, and 1971. Pakistan also did not formally declare war during the May 2025 crisis (only calling India's actions an "act of war"). The resolution criteria strictly require a formal declaration, which goes beyond military strikes, surgical operations, or ceasefire violations. With the conflict having de-escalated by the knowledge cutoff date (October 2025), the probability of a formal declaration by December 31, 2025 is extremely low.

Elon Musk explicitly committed to remaining Tesla CEO for the next 5 years at the Qatar Economic Forum in May 2025. The Tesla board confirmed confidence in him. Reports of a CEO search were denied by Tesla. He stepped down from DOGE but remained Tesla CEO. The only scenarios for YES would be death, forced removal, or a dramatic reversal — all extremely unlikely given his strong, recent public commitment and board support. Probability is very low, around 4%.

As of October 26, 2025, Tesla's Austin robotaxi service has safety monitors in every car. The resolution requires Waymo-style (no human in car) driverless operation with 10,000+ qualifying rides by December 31, 2025. All rides accumulated so far don't count because of the no-observer requirement. Musk said on Oct 22-23 he aims to remove safety monitors "by end of year," and Tesla is hiring Rapid Response operators to replace in-car monitors. However: (1) Musk has a track record of missed timelines; (2) NHTSA probe creates regulatory risk; (3) even if monitors removed in November, Tesla would need to scale rides quickly with only ~30 cars; (4) removal in December would likely leave insufficient time to hit 10,000 rides. I estimate ~30-35% chance monitors are removed before year end AND sufficient rides accumulate, but the combined probability with ride scale is approximately 12%.

South Korea's 2024 TFR was already 0.75 (above the 0.72 baseline). In 2025, Q1 TFR was 0.82, H1 births were up 7.4% year-on-year (record growth), and monthly births rose through at least August 2025. The annual 2025 TFR will almost certainly exceed 0.72. The main uncertainty is whether official 2025 annual data will be available by the January 1, 2026 resolution date (Statistics Korea typically releases this in February/March), which could delay resolution but not change the underlying outcome. Given the overwhelming positive trend and slight timing uncertainty, I estimate ~0.88 probability of YES resolution.

Elon Musk remains Tesla CEO as of late October 2025. Despite a May 2025 WSJ report about a board-led CEO search (which Tesla denied), Musk publicly committed to leading Tesla for 5 more years in May 2025, and the board proposed a massive $1 trillion compensation package for him in September 2025. All evidence points to Musk staying on as CEO through 2025 and beyond. The probability of him ceasing to be CEO by December 31, 2025 is very low.

The US did directly strike Iran on June 21, 2025 (nuclear sites), Iran retaliated against a US base on June 23, and Trump announced a ceasefire on June 24 ending the "12-Day War." Multiple major news outlets said "US enters war with Iran" and Trump himself declared US was "directly into the war" - meeting the "public acknowledgement" and "media coverage" criteria. However, the creator's resolution criteria specifically excluded "very limited rounds of very targeted strikes" and required a "sustained campaign" or ground troops. The US strikes were described as "narrowly tailored" and the entire US involvement lasted only ~3 days before ceasefire. The ceasefire held through at least October 2025 (cutoff). The ambiguity between the media/official characterization of "war" versus the creator's strict "sustained conflict" criteria creates genuine uncertainty. Given the overwhelming media coverage calling it a war, Trump's own statements, and Iran's acknowledgment, I estimate ~62% probability that the creator would resolve YES, recognizing the creator noted that strong media language "could tip scenarios towards YES."

San Diego FC, an MLS expansion team in their first season, not only made the 2025 MLS playoffs but earned the #1 seed in the Western Conference with 63 points. Multiple sources from October 2025 confirm they are in the playoffs and had a playoff game on October 19, 2025. The question resolves YES with near certainty.

All evidence conclusively shows no human moon landing will occur by end of 2025. Artemis II (crewed lunar flyby, no landing) is scheduled for April 2026, and Artemis III (actual lunar landing) is now targeting mid-2027 after multiple delays. No other nation or private entity has a credible crewed moon landing mission scheduled for 2025. The probability is essentially zero.

TSLA is trading around $430-$439 as of late October 2025, having already survived the worst of its 2025 selloff (bottoming around $220-250 in April 2025). To drop below $200 would require a ~54% decline in just 2 months, which would be extraordinary. The stock has been recovering strongly since April, turned positive for 2025 in September, and is near YTD highs. There is no evidence of impending catastrophic events that would cause such a massive decline. Probability is very low (~3%).

The current (September 2025) CPI inflation rate is 3.0% year-over-year, up from 2.9% in August, and the trend is flat to rising. Q4 2025 forecasts from J.P. Morgan, MNI, and market consensus suggest inflation staying around 3.0-3.5%. There are no credible forecasts predicting a drop below 2.5% by December 2025, and tariff-related pressures under the Trump administration are expected to keep inflation elevated or push it higher. While inflation briefly dipped to ~2.4% in May 2025, it quickly rebounded. Given current data and all projections, the probability that inflation will be below 2.5% by December 10, 2025 is very low — approximately 5%.

The Nobel Peace Prize 2025 went to María Corina Machado (NOT Trump), so Nobel=NO is confirmed. Given this, the market resolves YES only if there's NO bilateral ceasefire before 2026. As of Oct 21-22, 2025 (days before the knowledge cutoff), Russia explicitly rejected Trump's ceasefire proposal along current front lines, Putin-Trump summit was put on hold, and negotiations were deadlocked with Russia demanding full control of eastern Donbas. With only ~2 months remaining until the Jan 1, 2026 deadline, a bilateral ceasefire is unlikely (~12% chance). Therefore, P(market resolves YES) ≈ 87%.

No evidence of a Waymo SNL sketch in all available 2025 episodes through October 2025. Season 50 (Jan-May) and Season 51 premiere episodes show no Waymo content in any recaps or best-of lists. With only ~2-3 episodes remaining before the January 1, 2026 resolution date, and no indication SNL is planning such a sketch, the probability is very low. The base rate for any specific company getting an SNL sketch is small, and while Waymo is culturally relevant, it hasn't had a major viral moment that would propel it to SNL attention.

Elon Musk declared "Today, the America Party is formed" on July 5, 2025, following his feud with Trump over the Big Beautiful Bill. The party has a dedicated website and X account (300K+ followers), and major media (Reuters, NYT, CNN, BBC, CNBC, NPR) universally covered it as a new political party. This satisfies the resolution criteria requiring more than just a tweet, including a website/social media and media consensus. However, by late August 2025, Musk reportedly put brakes on the party to refocus on Tesla/SpaceX, creating uncertainty about whether the creator would consider it "officially established" enough. The community-run X account saying "No official affiliation" also adds ambiguity about Musk being the "official founding member." Balancing the clear formation announcement with tangible infrastructure against the subsequent cooling off, I estimate approximately 0.72 probability of YES resolution.

As of late October 2025, Iran's regime faces unprecedented pressures: a devastating 12-day war with Israel that killed IRGC commanders and damaged nuclear facilities, Khamenei's health concerns and month-long public absence, widespread economic protests, and significant internal factionalism. However, Khamenei returned with a speech on October 20, 2025, the IRGC maintains security control, and multiple expert analyses (CFR, Brandeis) conclude the regime remains resilient. Iran has survived all previous upheavals (2009, 2019, 2022). With only ~2 months remaining in 2025, the probability of complete regime collapse (losing control of Tehran's central government) in that narrow window is very low - around 4%.

All evidence through October 2025 confirms LLM hallucinations have NOT been solved and remain a critical open challenge. OpenAI's own September 2025 paper proved hallucinations may be mathematically impossible to fully eliminate. GPT-5 reduced hallucinations but didn't eliminate them. Academic surveys from August-October 2025 unanimously describe hallucinations as an ongoing problem. There is no evidence of a breakthrough that would "fix" hallucinations by January 1, 2026. The probability of YES resolution is extremely low (~3%).

The lawsuit between Pxie and Destiny (filed February 2025) was still in active litigation as of October 4, 2025, with new motions being filed. Both parties appear adversarial with no indication of settlement talks. Destiny has high-profile defense counsel and believes the case favors him based on Pxie's court testimony. With only ~2 months remaining until the January 1, 2026 resolution date, and the case still in active discovery/motions phase, settlement by year-end seems unlikely (~10%).

Nigeria's bank account penetration rate was only 52% as of 2023 (CBN data), and the broader financial inclusion rate (including mobile money and informal services) was only 74% in 2023. Even by 2024, the financial inclusion rate was still around 64-74% depending on the metric. This is dramatically below the 95% threshold required for YES resolution. The CBN's own 95% target for 2024 was widely acknowledged to have been missed. There is essentially no chance this question resolves YES.

No binding international treaty restricting solar geoengineering exists or is close to completion as of October 2025. The Solar Geoengineering Non-Use Agreement remains an academic/civil society initiative without sovereign state signatories. AMCEN resolutions and individual country statements of opposition are political declarations, not treaty obligations. UNEA-6 failed to pass even a non-binding resolution on the topic. The current advocacy goal is a UN General Assembly resolution (also non-binding). There is no active intergovernmental negotiating process that could produce a ratified treaty by December 31, 2025. The probability of 10+ countries making formal treaty-level commitments in this timeframe is extremely low.

The resolution criteria require a peer-reviewed systematic review from at least 3 independent labs establishing with 95% confidence both that recovered material is non-terrestrial AND has a technological origin, published before January 1, 2026. Multiple independent scientific analyses (ScienceDirect January 2025, The Conversation May 2025, 2024 LPSC) are actively contradicting even Loeb's more modest claims about interstellar origin, let alone technological origin. The scientific consensus is moving against Loeb's conclusions. No study has come close to establishing technological origin. The timeline is too tight for a potential summer 2025 expedition's results to be analyzed and published with peer review before January 2026. The probability is at the minimum bound.

The Magnificent Seven hit a combined all-time high of $20.9 trillion on October 9, 2025, just weeks before the knowledge cutoff. For the question to resolve YES, at least 4 of 7 stocks must close 50%+ below their all-time highs before January 1, 2026 - only ~2 months away. Even during the worst correction in 2025 (Q1 tariff crisis), the hardest-hit stock (Tesla) only fell ~45.5% from its ATH, and NVIDIA ~30%. A 50%+ drop in multiple mega-cap stocks within 2 months from near all-time highs would require a historically unprecedented catastrophe. No such event was occurring as of the knowledge cutoff, making resolution YES virtually impossible.

The current US death toll from antibiotic-resistant infections is approximately 35,000 per year (per CDC and PACCARB data as of 2025). The question asks if this will double to 70,000 by December 31, 2025. No credible source has reported anything approaching this figure. The rise from 23,000 (2013) to 35,000 was partly methodological. Global AMR projections show gradual increases over decades, not sudden doubling within a year or two. There is essentially no evidence this threshold will be reached by end of 2025, making this extremely unlikely.

Rockstar Games officially announced on May 2, 2025 that GTA VI has been delayed from its originally planned Fall 2025 window to May 26, 2026. Multiple major sources (Variety, ESPN, GamingBible) confirm this delay. The resolution date is November 30, 2025, well before the new release date of May 26, 2026. Therefore, GTA VI will almost certainly NOT be available for sale in Europe in 2025. A small residual probability remains for extraordinary circumstances (e.g., surprise early release, which is essentially impossible given a confirmed delay announcement).

Bitcoin is currently trading around $111,000-$114,000 as of late October 2025, which is approximately 20% above the resolution threshold of $93,429.20. The resolution date is November 30, 2025. For this to resolve NO, Bitcoin would need to fall ~20% in approximately 5 weeks - a severe but not impossible correction. The 2025 bull market has been strong (ATH above $126,000 in early October), institutional demand remains robust, and the pro-crypto regulatory environment under Trump supports continued strength. While Bitcoin volatility could cause a significant drop, the 20% buffer provides substantial protection against a NO resolution. Historical patterns and current market momentum strongly favor a YES resolution.

As of approximately October 13-14, 2025, no storm exceeded 160 mph. The two Category 5 storms (Erin at 160 mph in August and Humberto at 160 mph in late September) both tied but did not exceed that threshold. With only ~2 weeks remaining until resolution (October 27), the season was winding down. Jerry dissipated as a tropical storm, Karen quickly became post-tropical, and Lorenzo was just forming. Late October is typically less favorable for intense cyclogenesis in the Atlantic. While the Caribbean and Gulf remain warm, the probability of any storm rapidly intensifying beyond 160 mph in this short window appears low, estimated at approximately 12%.

Chegg faces serious business challenges from AI disruption, with revenue declining ~36% YoY. However, as of Q2 2025, the company had $114M in cash and investments vs. only ~$62M in remaining convertible notes (2026 maturity), giving a positive net cash balance of ~$52M. They already repaid $358.9M in notes that matured in March 2025. They're generating positive adjusted EBITDA and have engaged Goldman Sachs to explore strategic alternatives including a sale or go-private transaction. For bankruptcy to occur before January 1, 2026, an extremely rapid liquidity crisis would need to emerge in the remaining ~2 months. Given the net cash positive position, ongoing strategic review process, and absence of imminent debt maturities, bankruptcy before January 1, 2026 is very unlikely but not impossible.

The main LHCb anomaly (3.1σ in RK) that motivated this question was resolved in December 2022 when updated measurements showed RK = 0.949 ± 0.047, fully consistent with Standard Model predictions. No other lepton universality violation measurement has approached 5σ significance as of the knowledge cutoff (October 2025). Continued testing through 2024-2025 has yielded no breakthrough results. With only ~2 months remaining before the December 30, 2025 resolution date and no known candidate measurement approaching 5σ, the probability of this resolving YES is very low.

Mikie Sherrill leads NJ governor polling by ~5-7 points in most late October surveys (Rutgers-Eagleton +5, FDU +7, Fox News +8, Quinnipiac +6), with one outlier showing a dead heat (Emerson). Prediction markets assess her at 78-84% to win. New Jersey leans Democratic in statewide races, giving her structural advantage, though Ciattarelli nearly won the last NJ governor race in 2021. The race has tightened from double-digit leads, suggesting some risk, but the consistent polling lead and market consensus support ~82% probability.

FY 2025 (ended September 30, 2025) customs duties are estimated at approximately $195 billion by the CBO - far below the $500 billion lower bound of this bracket. The actual collections are well-documented and consistent across multiple sources. There is essentially no chance that the Treasury's Financial Report will show customs duties between $500b-$1t for FY 2025.

As of early October 2025, Claude 4.5 Sonnet held #1 on LMArena, which is positive for Anthropic. However, the leaderboard is highly volatile, changing roughly every 4-8 weeks. With ~3 months until December 31, strong competitors (OpenAI, Google, xAI) are likely to release new models that could displace Claude. Historical pattern shows the top spot rotating frequently among Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google throughout 2025. Given roughly 4-5 competitive players, Anthropic's current lead, and the long time horizon, I estimate approximately 27% probability that Anthropic holds #1 on December 31, 2025. OpenAI in particular has a track record of year-end releases, and Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro was also dominant just weeks before Claude 4.5 Sonnet took over.

Johannes Kaiser is polling in 4th place at approximately 12% as of late October 2025, compared to frontrunner Jeannette Jara at ~30% and Kast at ~23%. For Kaiser to win the first round (receive the most valid votes), he would need an unprecedented polling error of nearly 18-20 percentage points. This is essentially impossible given the large gap between him and the leading candidate, his organizational limitations, and the lack of any evidence pointing to a massive last-minute surge. The probability is set at the minimum meaningful level of 0.02.

The Brooklyn Nets are in a complete rebuild for 2025-26, having drafted five rookies in the first round (unprecedented), with analysts' best-case scenario being "finishing with the worst record in the NBA." Additionally, the resolution date of March 13, 2026 is before the NBA playoffs even begin - the Eastern Conference Finals happen in May/June 2026. The market would either resolve "No" if the Nets are mathematically eliminated from playoff contention by March 13 (very likely given their rebuild status), or "Other" since the ECF winner won't be announced by that date. There is essentially no probability of this resolving YES.

Moonshot AI (Kimi) has shown no evidence of being competitive at the top of the Chatbot Arena leaderboard. As of August 2025, the top positions are firmly held by models from OpenAI (GPT-5), xAI (Grok 4), Google (Gemini 2.5 Pro), and Anthropic (Claude). There is no evidence of Moonshot appearing in any top-ranking positions on the leaderboard, and with only 5 days between the knowledge cutoff and resolution date, there is virtually no chance of a sudden reversal. The probability is very low (~3%).

Trump being removed via Section 4 of the 25th Amendment by December 31, 2025 is essentially impossible for multiple converging reasons: (1) No president has ever been removed this way historically; (2) VP Vance and Cabinet are Trump loyalists with no indication of invoking Section 4; (3) Even if initiated, a 2/3 majority in both chambers would be needed if Trump contests, which is mathematically near-impossible with Republican majorities; (4) Calls for invoking the 25th Amendment are coming only from Democrats who have no power under Section 4; (5) Trump appears to be functioning normally as president. The probability is at the minimum threshold of 0.02.

Russia invading Finland in 2025 is extremely unlikely for multiple reasons: (1) Finland is a NATO member since 2023, triggering Article 5 collective defense if attacked; (2) Russia is deeply committed to its war in Ukraine and lacks troops near the Finnish border for a conventional invasion; (3) Finnish and NATO military officials consistently state there is no immediate military threat; (4) Analysts assess large-scale Russian attack on Finland as unlikely through 2025-27; (5) Russia's military is stretched thin and rebuilding would take years post-Ukraine. While Russia is building up military infrastructure near the Finnish border for long-term strategic positioning, this does not constitute an imminent invasion threat. The probability of an actual invasion by December 31, 2025 is at the minimum credible level.

The 12-month CPI has ranged from 2.3% to 3.0% throughout all of 2025, with September 2025 showing 3.0%. To reach 4.1% or more in any remaining month (Oct, Nov, Dec 2025), inflation would need to jump over 1.1 percentage points in one month - requiring monthly price increases of roughly 1.2%+, far beyond any monthly reading seen in 2025 (max ~0.7% in January). This is extremely unlikely barring an extraordinary economic shock, making this question almost certain to resolve NO.

The Kansas City Chiefs are one of the top Super Bowl contenders for 2026. They started the 2025 season 0-2 but recovered to above .500 by Week 8 (as of October 21, 2025) and are ranked in the top 3-7 in power rankings. Sportsbook odds had them at +850 (4th best in the NFL) as of August 2025, implying approximately 10.5% probability. With Patrick Mahomes and Andy Reid, they remain perennial contenders, but face stiff competition from teams like the Eagles, Bills, and Lions. The Chiefs' recovery from 0-2 and their strong power rankings suggest they're still legitimate contenders, but given the competitive NFL landscape with ~8-10 realistic contenders for the title, a probability around 13% seems well-calibrated.

Matt Salsberg is confirmed as one of 64 participants in the 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship. The base probability for any single player is ~1.6%. While there is evidence that Polymarket markets were showing one player at near 100% odds (suggesting an insider leak of the result), I could not confirm from accessible sources that this player was Salsberg. He is a solid but not elite poker pro. Adjusting upward slightly from base rate for uncertainty about the leaked winner's identity, I estimate approximately 12% probability.

Avatar: Fire and Ash (releasing Dec 19, 2025) faces two hurdles: first being nominated (estimated ~35% chance based on early predictions favoring technical categories over Best Picture), then winning (estimated ~10% even if nominated, given historical precedent - neither Avatar film has won Best Picture despite nominations, and strong competition from other contenders like "One Battle After Another," Frankenstein, Hamnet, etc.). Combined probability ~3.5%, rounded up to the minimum allowed 0.05.

Eminem is not a realistic contender for top Spotify artist of 2025. He accumulated ~6 billion streams in 2025 (through October), ranking around 22nd most-listened-to artist on the platform. The top contenders — Taylor Swift (betting favorite at 2/5 odds, two-time defending champion with ~26.6B streams in 2024), The Weeknd (new album + major tour), Bruno Mars, and Drake — are all dramatically outstreaming Eminem. The gap between Eminem's projected annual streams and those of the likely winner is roughly 4x or more. Eminem has no realistic path to winning Spotify Wrapped 2025.

Article 5 has only been invoked once in NATO's history (9/11, 2001). No evidence of any invocation in 2025 through my knowledge cutoff (Oct 26, 2025). The 2025 NATO Summit reaffirmed Article 5 without invoking it. Discussions are about defense spending and Ukraine guarantees, not an actual collective defense trigger. With ~2 months remaining and no known imminent trigger, the probability is extremely low (~2%).

Billie Eilish is very unlikely to be the top Spotify artist for 2025. Key evidence: (1) Taylor Swift dominated in 2024 and released a new album in October 2025 that broke single-day streaming records on Spotify; (2) Expert analysis from August 2025 identifies Taylor Swift and The Weeknd as the main contenders, with no mention of Billie Eilish; (3) No major new Billie Eilish release in 2025 has been reported; (4) Billie Eilish's "Hit Me Hard and Soft" ranked second in album streams in 2024 but she didn't win top artist even then. The probability of her being the #1 most streamed artist in 2025 is extremely low.

Key factors: (1) Strong contractual disincentives - an official AGI declaration would restrict Microsoft's access to technology per their agreement; (2) A "vibe shift" as of August 2025 shows AI leaders pulling back from AGI talk; (3) No official board declaration found despite 10 months of 2025 having passed; (4) Only ~2 months remain in 2025 after the knowledge cutoff. The probability of an official AGI announcement by December 31, 2025 is extremely low.

I estimate approximately 57% probability of YES - leaning toward launch happening in the Nov 3-24 window based on the project's momentum and commitment, but with meaningful downside risk from potential delays.

No evidence of WHO declaring a new coronavirus pandemic in 2025. The bat coronavirus HKU5-CoV-2 was identified as having human infection potential but is not a current public health threat. As of October 26, 2025 (knowledge cutoff), no new coronavirus pandemic has been declared, and only ~2 months remain in the resolution window. Base rates for such declarations are extremely low, and none of the newly identified bat coronaviruses have caused documented human outbreaks.

October 2025 global temperature anomaly (NASA GISS, vs 1951-1980 baseline) is likely near but somewhat above the 1.10°C threshold. Evidence: May 2025 was 1.07°C (just below threshold), September 2025 Copernicus data converts to ~1.17°C above 1951-1980 (above threshold), and 2025 is running warm despite La Niña cooling. The Hansen et al. 2025 paper suggests temperatures near 1.2°C above 1951-1980 going forward. There's also uncertainty about whether NASA GISS will release October data before the November 11 resolution date (historically ~10-12 days after month end, so ~75-80% probability). Combined probability: ~35% chance temp < 1.10°C × ~80% data available = ~28%.

As of the October 26, 2025 knowledge cutoff, the Kansas City Chiefs are 4-3 in the AFC West, sitting one game behind the Denver Broncos (5-2) who are on a 4-game winning streak. The LA Chargers (4-3) are also tied with the Chiefs. This is the most competitive the AFC West has been in years, with a genuine 3-team race. While the Chiefs have won 10 consecutive AFC West titles with Mahomes/Reid, they face a real deficit. With ~10 games remaining, they have plenty of time to overcome the gap, but the Broncos are playing their best football in years. I estimate ~38% probability the Chiefs win the division, reflecting their current one-game deficit balanced against their historical excellence and experience in competitive division races.

The September 2025 FOMC meeting confirmed a 25bps cut (to 4.00%-4.25%). For CUT-PAUSE-CUT to occur, October must be a PAUSE and December must be a CUT. As of October 23, 2025, CME FedWatch shows 99% probability of a 25bps October cut (not a pause), with multiple Fed officials explicitly supporting another cut. The probability of an October pause is ~1%, and if October were a pause, December would be nearly certain to be a cut (~99%). Overall probability ≈ 1% × 99% ≈ ~1%, rounded up slightly to 0.02 to account for uncertainty.

Moonshot's Kimi-K2 was ranked 8th (tied) on the LMArena overall leaderboard as of September 2025, with the top positions held by Google (Gemini 2.5 Pro), OpenAI (GPT-5), and xAI (Grok). For Moonshot to capture the #1 spot by December 31, 2025, it would need to leap 7+ positions while competing against much better-resourced US companies that are continually releasing new models. While Kimi-K2 is impressive (top open-source model), the gap to the overall #1 position is substantial. The probability is very low, estimated at approximately 4%.

The October 2025 GISS Land-Ocean Temperature Index anomaly is expected to fall in the range of approximately 0.9-1.3°C based on 2025 trends (May 2025 was 1.07°C, and 2025 is running 0.1-0.3°C cooler than 2024's El Niño-driven peak values). The specific bracket of 1.10-1.14°C spans only 5 centidegrees in this distribution. While this bracket is plausible and near the center of the likely range, the chance of landing precisely in this narrow window is about 15-20%. There's also uncertainty about whether NASA will release October data before the November 11 resolution date.

Multiple sources from mid-to-late October 2025 show the CME FedWatch Tool pricing in a 97-99% probability of a 25 bps rate cut at the October 29, 2025 FOMC meeting. The Fed had already cut by 25 bps in September 2025 (11-1 vote), and the September FOMC projections (dot plot) indicated two more cuts through year-end 2025. All forecasters and market indicators point to a 25 bps cut bringing the rate from 4.00%-4.25% to 3.75%-4.00%. Given the near-consensus expectation, I estimate a 96% probability of YES.

All available polls through October 2025 show Mamdani firmly in first place (43-44%) with a 14+ point lead over Cuomo (28-29%), who is in second. Sliwa is third (~19%). For the question to resolve YES (Mamdani winning second place), he would need to fall from first to second in the final week of campaigning. Given the consistency and magnitude of his lead across multiple polls from different pollsters, this is very unlikely. The probability he finishes second rather than first is estimated at approximately 8%.

Le Havre barely survived relegation in 2024-25 and is considered a bottom-half survival team in 2025-26. PSG has a 78.7% chance of winning the title (Opta), having dominated Ligue 1 for years. Le Havre is not mentioned as any kind of title contender. By the March 1, 2026 resolution date (~matchday 26), Le Havre will almost certainly be mathematically eliminated from the title race, resolving this market "No". The probability of a YES outcome is negligible.

Walter Reeves is a fringe challenger to incumbent Mayor Andre Dickens in the 2025 Atlanta mayoral election. Dickens has significant advantages in money, name recognition, and incumbency. Reeves previously ran in 2021 without success, and there's no evidence of significant polling support, fundraising, or campaign momentum in 2025. All reporting describes Dickens as the overwhelming frontrunner with only minor challengers. The probability of Reeves winning is extremely low.

As of my knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), xAI appears NOT to hold the top spot on the Chatbot Arena leaderboard. Grok 3 briefly led in February 2025, but by August 2025, GPT-5 (OpenAI) topped the leaderboard, with Google's Gemini 2.5 Pro also being a strong contender. For xAI to win at December 31, they would need to either currently hold the top spot or release a new competitive model in the remaining 2 months. Given the current trajectory (OpenAI and Google ahead), the competitive pressure, and the volatility of the leaderboard, I estimate approximately 20% probability that xAI holds the top spot on December 31, 2025.

The September 2025 FOMC meeting confirmed a 25 bps rate cut (first qualifying event). For "cut-pause-pause" to resolve YES, the October 28-29 meeting must be a PAUSE and December must also be a PAUSE. However, as of October 26, CME FedWatch shows 99% probability of a cut at the October 29 meeting (not a pause), and 115 of 117 economists surveyed by Reuters also predict a cut. FOMC September minutes confirmed expectations for two more cuts in 2025 (October and December). P(cut-pause-pause) ≈ P(Oct=pause) × P(Dec=pause|Oct=pause) ≈ 1% × ~30% = ~0.3%, with ~3% accounting for uncertainty.

As of October 26, 2025 (the knowledge cutoff), there has been no documented military engagement between US and Venezuelan military forces (FANB, coast guard, or militia), despite months of extreme tension. The US has conducted at least 5 kinetic strikes in the region, but all targeted alleged narco vessels in international waters, not Venezuelan military assets. Venezuela has not retaliated militarily. Only ~5 days remain in the August 21 - October 31 resolution window. The pattern is one of coercive signaling rather than actual armed conflict. The B-1 flights on October 24 and the overall build-up signal pressure, but both sides appear to be deliberately avoiding direct military-to-military engagement. Given the extremely short remaining window and the established pattern of avoiding direct military contact despite extreme provocation, the probability of a qualifying engagement is low but non-trivial given the unprecedented tension level.

While the US has conducted significant military operations near Venezuela (10+ maritime strikes, massive naval buildup, plans for land strikes announced Oct 21), none of this meets the resolution criteria of "military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Venezuela." The US actions are framed as counter-narcotics operations, not territorial conquest. Experts consistently assess a full-scale invasion as very unlikely. There are ~2 months remaining in the resolution window, but escalating from strike operations to territorial occupation would require a dramatic shift in policy, would face enormous domestic and international opposition, and would be logistically extremely challenging. The probability is very low but non-negligible given the unprecedented military buildup and Trump's aggressive posture.

Israel and Iran agreed to a ceasefire around June 24, 2025 after a 12-day conflict. The ceasefire has been holding for approximately 4 months as of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025). No confirmed new direct strikes were found in my research after the ceasefire. While analysts in early October 2025 warned about the risk of a second Israeli strike, the predicted "imminent" attack window (September-October 7) passed without confirmed strikes. Diplomatic pressure from Trump and the holding ceasefire reduce but don't eliminate the probability. With only 5 days remaining until resolution, and no evidence of a fresh attack, I estimate roughly 18% probability that a qualifying strike occurs or has already occurred.

The US conducted targeted airstrikes against Iranian nuclear facilities (Operation Midnight Hammer) on June 22, 2025, but this was an air campaign, not a ground invasion intended to establish territorial control. A ceasefire was reached by June 24, 2025. There is no evidence of any US ground forces entering Iranian territory or any US military action designed to "establish control over any portion of Iran." The resolution criteria requires a military offensive intended to control Iranian territory, which did not occur. The probability is very low (~3%) accounting for remote uncertainty about events between late October and December 2025.

The Weeknd had a strong 2025 (new album release, Blinding Lights hitting 5B streams, reclaimed #1 monthly listeners) but faces Taylor Swift as the dominant incumbent who won Spotify Wrapped in 2023 and 2024. Betting markets in January 2025 gave The Weeknd ~25% implied probability vs Taylor Swift's ~71%. His strong 2025 performance (new album, high monthly listener counts) slightly increases his odds above the January baseline, but Taylor Swift's structural advantages (massive catalog, devout fanbase, historically dominant annual streaming) remain substantial. Bruno Mars was also strong early in 2025. Estimating ~28% probability for The Weeknd to win Spotify Wrapped 2025.

Polymarket US launched on a CFTC-regulated Designated Contract Market (DCM) in early October 2025. The company acquired QCEX (a CFTC-licensed exchange) for $112 million in July 2025, received regulatory clearance in September 2025, and began listing markets for US users starting October 2, 2025. Multiple credible sources confirm the relaunch with real-money trading. This clearly satisfies the resolution criteria well before December 31, 2025.

The US conducted aerial strikes on Iran (Operation Midnight Hammer, June 21-22, 2025) using B-2 bombers, but all operations were aerial - entering Iranian airspace, not terrestrial territory. VP Vance explicitly stated "no interest" in ground troops in Iran. A ceasefire was brokered around June 24, 2025. No credible reporting exists of US ground forces physically entering Iranian territory. The resolution criteria specifically exclude aerial/maritime operations, SOF, advisors, and contractors. The probability of regular US combat troops physically entering Iran's terrestrial territory between June 21 and December 31, 2025 is extremely low.

A Minecraft Movie opened to approximately $162-163M domestically (final figures), making it the biggest opening weekend of 2025 as of October 2025. No film released through October surpassed it (Lilo & Stitch at ~$146M was closest). The main remaining threats are Wicked: For Good (projected $130-155M range, still below Minecraft), Zootopia 2 (Thanksgiving Wednesday opener, 3-day figures likely lower), and Avatar: Fire and Ash (December). There remains approximately 20-22% chance one of these late 2025 releases - primarily Wicked: For Good with its record pre-sales - could outperform expectations and surpass Minecraft's opening.

Gemini 2.5 Pro has held the #1 position on Chatbot Arena since March 2025 and remains #1 as of late October 2025 (just before the knowledge cutoff). The AI space evolves rapidly, and competitive pressures are high. Weighing the stability of Gemini's dominance (~7 months) against the risk of a new breakthrough model in Nov-Dec 2025, I estimate a ~65% probability that Google will still have the top model on December 31, 2025.

As of October 26, 2025 (knowledge cutoff), WHO has not declared any disease a pandemic in 2025. The WHO Pandemic Agreement adoption is about preparedness, not a declaration. The mpox PHEIC was ended in September 2025, and H5N1 bird flu has not even reached PHEIC status. WHO is historically very reluctant to use the term "pandemic" - having only done so once officially (COVID-19 in March 2020).

Starmer remains PM as of late October 2025 despite very low approval ratings (~13%) and internal party pressure. Key factors preventing his removal before December 31, 2025: (1) Labour's formal leadership challenge mechanism cannot be triggered until the 2026 party conference cycle; (2) Labour's massive parliamentary majority (~412 seats) makes a no-confidence vote impossible; (3) the main potential challenger (Andy Burnham) is not an MP and thus ineligible; (4) Starmer has actively dismissed resignation calls. While there is real political pressure, the structural barriers to his removal within the timeframe are very high, making a ~5% probability appropriate.

John Smith is a legitimate participant described as "King of the Heads-Up Tournaments" with 60 years of poker experience - a genuine heads-up specialist. However, the 64-player field includes many elite professional players. The Polymarket market converged to "nearly 100%" for one player (based on insider knowledge of the pre-recorded event), but I was unable to confirm whether that player is John Smith. Given the competition from players like Doug Polk, Phil Galfond, Michael Mizrachi, and other heads-up specialists, combined with Smith's base rate of ~1.6% and his specialized skill advantage, I estimate approximately 12% probability - above base rate due to his heads-up expertise, but uncertain without knowing the identity of the Polymarket favorite.

Z.ai is a Chinese AI company (Zhipu AI) making GLM-series models. As of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025), the Chatbot Arena leaderboard has been dominated by OpenAI (GPT-5 was #1 as of August 2025), with Google, Anthropic, and xAI competing closely behind. Z.ai has no established history of leading the Chatbot Arena leaderboard, and their models don't appear in any recent top rankings. The probability they will have the #1 arena score on October 31, 2025 is very low.

Federal government current expenditures (FGEXPND) were approximately $7,109.85 billion SAAR in Q4 2024. For YES resolution, Q4 2025 would need to show a $250B+ decrease to below ~$6,860 billion. All available evidence contradicts this:
1. Federal spending INCREASED ~$301B in FY2025 vs FY2024 (CBO data)
2. FY2025 deficit was $1.8 trillion; federal debt grew $2 trillion
3. DOGE's actual verified savings are tiny ($2-15B by independent estimates) vs. claimed figures
4. Federal agencies are actually rehiring and spending more
The spending trajectory is strongly upward, making a $250B+ decrease virtually impossible. Probability set at 0.03 (near minimum) to reflect near-certainty of NO resolution.

The Life of a Showgirl (Taylor Swift) debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 dated October 18, 2025, and had held that position for 3 consecutive weeks as of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025). The resolution date is December 2, 2025. By that date, the album could be at #1 for at most roughly 7 weeks. 17 consecutive weeks at #1 would require maintaining the top spot through approximately February 7, 2026, far beyond the resolution date. This makes it mathematically impossible for the question to resolve YES by December 2, 2025, so the probability is essentially 0 (minimum 0.02).

DeepSeek has not held the #1 overall position on the Chatbot Arena leaderboard throughout 2025 - they've topped the open-source sub-leaderboard but the overall top spot has been traded between OpenAI (GPT-4.5, GPT-5) and Google (Gemini 2.5 Pro). As of August 2025, GPT-5 topped lmarena's leaderboards. For DeepSeek to claim #1 by December 31, 2025, they'd need to release a breakthrough model surpassing GPT-5 and Gemini 2.5 Pro. While DeepSeek is capable (they surprised the market with V3/R1 in early 2025), the current competitive landscape and lack of signals that DeepSeek is close to the top spot make this unlikely. Probability estimated at ~8%.

Bitcoin has traded well above $100K throughout the entire July 14 - October 26, 2025 period, with a range of roughly $104K-$126K. As of October 26, BTC is at ~$113,840 - approximately 12.4% above the $100K trigger. With only ~9 days remaining before the November 4 resolution date, a 12.4% decline would be unusually large (nearly 1.4 standard deviations using 9-day volatility of ~9%). While analyst warnings about a potential sub-$100K dip exist, the $100K level represents strong psychological support. The probability of a barrier touch at -12.4% within 9 days is approximately 8-12%.

Prediction markets (Kalshi) as of August-September 2025 give Elon Musk approximately 6% probability of being named TIME's Person of the Year for 2025. The frontrunners are AI (32%), Pope Leo XIV (24%), Sam Altman (13%), ChatGPT (9%), and Donald Trump (10%). While Musk had significant influence via DOGE in early 2025, his profile has faded relative to AI-related candidates. He was already named Person of the Year in 2021, which slightly reduces his chances. Multiple independent prediction market sources consistently place him in the 5-8% range. A probability of ~7% reflects this market consensus.

Taylor Swift's "The Life of a Showgirl" debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 on the chart dated October 18, 2025 (released Oct. 3). As of the October 26 knowledge cutoff, the album had spent 3 consecutive weeks at #1. For 16 straight weeks at #1, chart data through approximately early February 2026 would be needed. By the resolution date of December 2, 2025, only about 7-8 chart weeks would have elapsed since the album's debut, making it mathematically impossible for 16 consecutive weeks to be confirmed by that date.

A Minecraft Movie set the 2025 domestic opening weekend record at $163 million. Zootopia 2 is projected for $95M-$130M in its 3-day opening (Nov 28-30). Even the most optimistic projections fall ~25% short of the $163M benchmark. For Zootopia 2 to win, it would need a dramatic overperformance beyond analyst expectations. While possible (as Minecraft Movie itself overperformed), it's unlikely for a Disney animated sequel over Thanksgiving where the 3-day excludes the lucrative Wednesday/Thursday Thanksgiving Day grosses. Probability estimated at ~12%.

The official charging documents state Robinson acted alone, and early law enforcement statements supported the lone actor theory. Physical evidence (grandfather's WWII rifle, DNA, text confessions) is strong. While the FBI under Kash Patel and Dan Bongino was investigating the "possibility of accomplices" through October 2025 (with IBTimes UK reporting on Oct 7 and 16 suggesting a conspiracy angle), no confirmed accomplices emerged before the knowledge cutoff. The investigation into accomplices appears to be politically motivated exploration rather than confirmed findings. If no definitive statement by October 31, the resolution based on "what law enforcement believe was most likely" would likely favor the lone actor conclusion supported by the charging documents. Probability set at 0.62 for YES (lone actor confirmed).

As of August 2025, OpenAI's GPT-5 topped the Chatbot Arena text leaderboard, but this is a highly dynamic competition. Throughout 2025, the top spot has switched multiple times between OpenAI (GPT-4.5, GPT-5) and Google (Gemini 2.5 Pro), with xAI (Grok) also competitive. Gemini 2.5 Pro held #1 for several months (roughly March-August 2025), while GPT-5 took #1 in August. Between the knowledge cutoff (Oct 26) and December 31, new models from Google, Anthropic, xAI, and OpenAI are likely to be released. Given 3-4 major competitors capable of holding #1, the fast pace of releases, and that Google has been clearly competitive throughout 2025 (and wins ties alphabetically), I estimate ~38% probability for OpenAI holding the top spot on December 31.

Bitcoin's lowest price in October 2025 was approximately $100,000-$108,000 during a flash crash on October 10th. By October 25-26, Bitcoin had recovered to ~$111,000-$114,000. For this to resolve YES, Bitcoin would need to drop ~20% to $90,000 in the remaining 5 days of October (Oct 27-31), which is extremely unlikely given current momentum and that the worst crash in October only reached ~$100k. No major catalysts point to such a severe drop.

Francesca Muñoz is the presidential candidate for the small Partido Social Cristiano (PSC). Polls showed her at only 1% voting intention in June 2025. The main contenders are Jeannette Jara (~26%), José Antonio Kast (~22%), and Evelyn Matthei (~14%). There are also indications she may have withdrawn and endorsed Kast. She has virtually no chance of winning the election.

As of October 26, 2025 (knowledge cutoff), no qualifying US-Venezuela military engagement has occurred despite 65+ days of intense US military operations. All US strikes targeted civilian drug trafficking boats in international waters, not Venezuelan military forces (FANB). The resolution window closes October 31, leaving only ~5 days. Trump signaled possible land strikes on October 24, but described them as future possibilities. Venezuela has put its military on maximum alert and conducted air defense drills, while the US deployed a carrier strike group. However, both sides have carefully avoided direct military-to-military confrontation throughout the campaign. The ~41% expert forecast for conflict by November likely includes scenarios that don't meet the strict resolution criteria (military-to-military engagement). Given only 5 days remaining, the ~20% probability reflects meaningful but contained escalation risk.

Valencia CF has negligible chance of winning the 2025-26 La Liga. The club has been fighting relegation in recent seasons with severe financial difficulties, while Real Madrid, Barcelona, and Atletico Madrid are the overwhelming title favorites. Furthermore, the resolution date of March 8, 2026 (around matchday 27-28) means the market would only resolve YES if Valencia clinched the title by that date - which almost never happens that early in a La Liga season. More likely, the market resolves NO because Valencia gets mathematically eliminated from title contention well before March 8. The probability is at the minimum viable level (~2%).

Taylor Swift's "The Life of a Showgirl" debuted at #1 on the Billboard 200 with a record 4.002 million units and spent at least 2 consecutive weeks at #1 as of the knowledge cutoff (October 26, 2025). The question resolves YES only if the album was #1 for 0 weeks, which is clearly not the case. Probability is at the minimum allowed value of 0.02.

Drake has never won Spotify Wrapped (most-streamed artist for a calendar year) despite being the all-time most-streamed artist. In 2025, he's the top rapper but faces stiff competition from Taylor Swift (2-time defending champion and betting favorite), The Weeknd (major album + world tour in 2025), and Bruno Mars (dominant in early 2025). Drake's annual stream pace (~7+ billion) historically falls short of the pop artists who win annual Wrapped. Betting markets don't list Drake as a top contender. His probability of winning Spotify Wrapped 2025 is approximately 6%.

No US ally (NATO member or Major Non-NATO Ally) is anywhere close to acquiring an independent nuclear weapon by end of 2025. South Korea is the most discussed candidate, but experts estimate it would take 10+ years from a political decision to achieve an operational arsenal. No credible reports indicate any US ally conducting nuclear weapons tests, acquiring sufficient fissile material, or reaching operational nuclear status. Technical and political barriers make this essentially impossible within the 2025 timeframe. The probability is set at the minimum threshold of 0.02.

On September 22, 2025, RFK Jr. and HHS made an official announcement linking prenatal Tylenol (acetaminophen) use and folate deficiencies to autism/ADHD, fulfilling his April pledge. This was a formal HHS press release widely reported by credible news outlets (CBS News, CNN, Axios). The announcement happened well before the October 31 deadline. While experts disputed the science and the language wasn't 100% "definitive" (using terms like "almost certainly"), the formal government announcement identifying causes of autism likely satisfies the resolution criteria. The main uncertainty is whether the market operator interprets "definitive cause" strictly enough to exclude this announcement.

Peru has been eliminated from CONMEBOL 2026 World Cup qualifying as of September 2025 (confirmed by multiple sources including Bolavip and World Soccer Talk). Multiple results explicitly state "Chile and Peru stand as the national teams eliminated from the competition." Since Peru cannot qualify for the 2026 FIFA World Cup, it is mathematically impossible for them to win it. Per the resolution criteria, this market resolves to "No" immediately when it becomes impossible for Peru to win. The probability is set at the minimum (0.02) rather than 0 to reflect the technical uncertainty, but effectively this is a near-certain "No" resolution.

Through September 2025, global temperatures are consistently tracking about 0.07-0.08°C below 2024's record pace. Multiple authoritative sources (Carbon Brief, WMO, Met Office, NOAA) project 2025 as the 2nd or 3rd warmest year, not the hottest. The transition from El Niño to La Niña/neutral conditions reduces warming potential compared to 2024. With 9 months of data showing a persistent deficit vs. 2024's record, it would require extraordinary warming in the final 3 months to close this gap. Probability of 2025 surpassing 2024's NASA GISTEMP record is low, estimated at ~10%.

Evidence shows xAI's Grok 4 was at #4 on LMArena as of July 2025, below Gemini 2.5 Pro and o3. GPT-5 then took #1 in August 2025. With strong competition from Google (Gemini), OpenAI (GPT-5), and Anthropic (Claude 4.5), and no evidence of a new xAI model recapturing #1 before October 31, the probability that xAI holds the top arena score on October 31, 2025 is approximately 10%.

As of October 22, 2025 (4 days before the knowledge cutoff and 9 days before resolution), Tesla's robotaxis in both Austin and San Francisco still require human safety monitors in the vehicle who can intervene. Elon Musk stated on October 22 that removing these monitors is targeted for "by the end of the year" (implying December 2025), not by October 31. The current system explicitly does not meet the resolution criteria (which requires no human required to monitor or intervene on-site). Given Tesla's consistent history of delayed timelines and the very short remaining window, the probability is very low.

Victor Ponta is not mentioned as a candidate or potential candidate in any reporting about the December 7, 2025 Bucharest mayoral by-election. The main candidates are Daniel Băluță (PSD, 25% in polls), Cătălin Drulă (USR, 18%), and Ciprian Ciucu (PNL, 18%). Ponta ran unsuccessfully in the May 2025 presidential race as an independent but has no reported connection to the mayoral race. The probability of him winning is negligibly low.

The September 2025 cut is confirmed (25bp to 4.00%-4.25%). The October 2025 cut is near-certain (~95-98% per CME FedWatch as of early October 2025). For Cut-Cut-Pause to resolve YES, the December meeting must be a pause. However, as of October 2025, markets priced ~90% probability of a December cut as well (not a pause), Reuters economists poll (Oct 21-22) expected cuts at both October AND December meetings, and Fed's own September dot plot signaled two more 2025 cuts. The dominant expectation is Cut-Cut-Cut. The probability of a December pause is approximately 10-15%, giving Cut-Cut-Pause roughly 10% probability.

SOL was trading around $191-196 as of October 23-26, 2025, with only 5 days remaining in the month. Reaching $350 would require an ~80% gain in days, far exceeding its all-time high of ~$294 set in January 2025 (which $350 would surpass by ~19%). All analyst forecasts for October 2025 peak prices were in the $190-268 range. There is essentially no realistic chance of reaching $350 by month end.

Bad Bunny has a very strong lead in 2025 Spotify streams - Rolling Stone reported he had 19.8 billion streams as the biggest artist of 2025 (mid-year), a substantial lead over competitors. His new album "DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS" has been a massive hit, and he maintained dominance throughout the year. Historical context shows he was #1 in 2020-2022 and is a perennial frontrunner. However, there's uncertainty because: (1) 2 months remain in the year, allowing The Weeknd, Taylor Swift, or others to catch up; (2) Taylor Swift was #1 in 2023 and potentially 2024; (3) there's some timing uncertainty about whether Spotify Wrapped 2025 will be released before the December 3 resolution date. Given the strong mid-year lead and continued momentum, I estimate ~68% probability.

Paul Biedermann's 200m LC freestyle world record (1:42.00, set in 2009 with a supersuit) is confirmed still standing on Wikipedia as of the knowledge cutoff (Oct 26, 2025). It has stood for 16+ years. The record is a supersuit-era record essentially unreachable with modern textile suits - the fastest recent textile swim (~Popovici's 1:43.21 in 2022) is ~1.2 seconds behind. Luke Hobson broke Biedermann's SHORT course record in Dec 2024, not the long course one. No major long course championships are scheduled during Nov 2025 - Jan 2026 (the 2025 LC Worlds already happened in Singapore in July 2025 without this record falling). Probability is very high (0.97, 0.96, 0.94 for the three dates) with slight decrease for longer horizons per instructions.

Viswanathan Anand is currently ranked 13th with 2743 rating. Key factors: (1) He is "semi-retired" and rarely plays classical chess, so his rating is essentially frozen. (2) Multiple active players are very close: Mamedyarov at 2742 (just -1), and 5 others within 6 points at 2737-2738. (3) With his rating fixed, any of those active players gaining enough rating points to surpass 2743 will push Anand lower than rank 13. (4) FIDE publishes monthly lists, so Nov 2 and Nov 25 both reflect the November 1 list. (5) Over 3 monthly cycles to January 2026, the chance that multiple active players have surpassed him increases significantly. Given these tight margins and the semi-retired status, I estimate: ~55% chance he maintains rank ≤13 in November, and ~45% for January 2026.
